words.def: in the direction of somebody/something
words.example: They were heading towards the German border.

words.def: a piece of cloth or paper used for drying things, especially your body
words.example: Help yourself to a clean towel.

words.def: a tall narrow building or part of a building, especially of a church or castle
words.example: a bell tower

words.def: made as a copy of a particular thing and used for playing with
words.example: a toy car

words.def: a belief, custom or way of doing something that has existed for a long time among a particular group of people; a set of these beliefs or customs
words.example: religious/cultural/ancient traditions

words.def: being part of the beliefs, customs or way of life of a particular group of people, that have not changed for a long time
words.example: traditional dress/music/art/culture/dance

words.def: the vehicles that are on a road at a particular time
words.example: There's always a lot of traffic at this time of day.

words.def: a number of connected coaches or trucks, pulled by an engine or powered by a motor in each one, taking people and goods from one place to another
words.example: to get on/off a train

words.def: the process of learning the skills that you need to do a job
words.example: staff training

words.def: the act or activity of travelling
words.example: air/rail/space travel

words.def: a person who is travelling or who often travels
words.example: She is a frequent traveller to Belgium.

words.def: a large vehicle for carrying heavy loads by road
words.example: a truck driver

words.def: two times; on two occasions
words.example: I don't know him well; I've only met him twice.

words.def: to know or realize the meaning of words, a language, what somebody says, etc.
words.example: understand (something), Can you understand French?

words.def: used to say that a particular situation or fact makes you sad or disappointed, or gets you into a difficult position
words.example: Unfortunately, I won't be able to attend the meeting.

words.def: not happy; sad
words.example: to feel/look/seem/sound/become unhappy

words.def: the special set of clothes worn by all members of an organization or a group at work, or by children at school
words.example: The hat is part of the school uniform.

words.def: a single thing, person or group that is complete by itself but can also form part of something larger
words.example: After 1946 the British Government treated the four territorial divisions as a single unit.

words.def: an institution at the highest level of education where you can study for a degree or do research
words.example: Is there a university in this town?

words.def: different from what is usual or normal
words.example: The case is highly unusual.

words.def: towards or in a higher position
words.example: He jumped up from his chair.

words.def: the act of using something; the state of being used
words.example: A ban was imposed on the use of chemical weapons.

words.def: that can help you to do or achieve what you want
words.example: a useful tool

words.def: that happens or is done most of the time or in most cases
words.example: This is the usual way of doing it.

words.def: an area of low land between hills or mountains, often with a river flowing through it; the land that a river flows through
words.example: in a valley, In the valley below cows were grazing peacefully.

words.def: a very small town located in a country area
words.example: We visited towns and villages all over Spain.

words.def: a person who visits a person or place
words.example: We've got visitors coming this weekend.

words.def: the sound or sounds produced through the mouth by a person speaking or singing
words.example: I could hear voices in the next room.

words.def: a situation in which two or more countries or groups of people fight against each other over a period of time
words.example: the Second World War

words.def: an act of cleaning somebody/something using water and usually soap
words.example: These towels are ready for a wash.

words.def: the act of cleaning something using water and usually soap
words.example: a gentle shampoo for frequent washing

words.def: not physically strong
words.example: She is still weak after her illness.

words.def: a system for finding information on the internet, in which documents are connected to other documents
words.example: to surf/browse/search the web

words.def: a set of pages on the internet, where a company or an organization, or an individual person, puts information
words.example: For current prices please visit our website.

words.def: a marriage ceremony, and the meal or party that usually follows it
words.example: I dreamed of having a big wedding, with all my family and friends.

words.def: how heavy somebody/something is, which can be measured in, for example, kilograms or pounds
words.example: in weight, It is about 76 kilos in weight.

words.def: in or towards the west
words.example: West Africa

words.def: covered with or containing liquid, especially water
words.example: wet clothes/hair/grass

words.def: one of the round objects under a car, bicycle, bus, etc. that turns when it moves
words.example: He braked suddenly, causing the front wheels to skid.

words.def: used in questions to ask who something belongs to
words.example: Whose house is that?

words.def: measuring a large distance from one side to the other
words.example: It's a wide, fast-flowing river.

words.def: living or growing in natural conditions; not kept in a house or on a farm
words.example: wild animals/birds/flowers

words.def: an alcoholic drink made from the juice of grapes that has been left to ferment. There are many different kinds of wine.
words.example: sparkling wine

words.def: a person, a team, an animal, etc. that wins something
words.example: The lucky winner gets an all-expenses-paid trip to Sydney.

words.def: used especially in a letter, email or card to say that you hope that somebody will be happy, well or successful
words.example: Give my good wishes to the family.

words.def: not having, experiencing or showing something
words.example: They had gone two days without food.

words.def: the hard material that the trunk and branches of a tree are made of; this material when it is used to build or make things with, or as a fuel
words.example: He chopped some wood for the fire.

words.def: made of wood
words.example: a wooden box/door/floor

words.def: thinking about unpleasant things that have happened or that might happen and therefore feeling unhappy and afraid
words.example: Don't look so worried!

words.def: used to show that you are very surprised or impressed by somebody/something
words.example: Wow! You look terrific!

words.def: the activity of writing, in contrast to reading, speaking, etc.
words.example: Our son’s having problems with his reading and writing (= at school)

words.def: of or belonging to you
words.example: Is that book yours?
Five, four, three, two, one, zero… We have lift-off.
